artifact: EC2 idle-shutdown watchdog — live self-stop certification
method: API-only observation: after the last SSH exec channel closed, state was polled exclusively via aws ec2 describe-instances every 300s (SSH would have reset the idle clock). No stop command was issued by any agent or human after disconnect_utc.
watchdog_version: infra/ec2-idle-shutdown/idle-shutdown.sh @ commit 9ee952c (ss-based SSH detection + PROOF_RUNNING sentinel)

timeline_utc.elapsed_disconnect_to_stop: ~35 min (spec: 30 min idle + up to one 5-min check cycle — IN SPEC)

api_evidence.reason_note: 'User initiated' is AWS's label for ANY OS-level shutdown (shutdown -h now inside the guest), as opposed to API StopInstances calls or AWS-side failures — consistent with the watchdog firing, not contradicting it.

verdict: CERTIFIED — the instance stopped ITSELF within the designed window with zero external intervention, observed entirely from outside the box.
